title: Evaluate Meshtastic and MeshCore HAL patterns for LICHEN Zephyr HAL
description: Why this exists: LICHEN should be strongly informed by Meshtastic and MeshCore hardware support because they already cover much of the same LoRa board ecosystem, but both are PlatformIO/Arduino-oriented and may encode board quirks in ways we should not copy directly into Zephyr. What needs to be done: review Meshtastic firmware board/variant/platform structure, MeshCore src/helpers/variants/target structure, radio/display/input/GNSS/power abstractions, app interface hooks, and build matrix. Identify which patterns are worth adapting to Zephyr devicetree/Kconfig and which are PlatformIO/Arduino artifacts to avoid. Acceptance criteria: source references and findings are captured in the Bead design or docs; reusable ideas are mapped to LICHEN HAL services; anti-patterns are listed explicitly; recommendations feed project-LICHEN-h7t5.1 HAL contract and project-LICHEN-h7t5.2 board matrix; two review passes before closure.
design:
Findings as of 2026-06-30:

Primary source refs:
- Meshtastic firmware repo HEAD checked at 87bb149afb9d9e17ef744aad5fbc010c3371f4dc: https://github.com/meshtastic/firmware/tree/87bb149afb9d9e17ef744aad5fbc010c3371f4dc
- Meshtastic platformio.ini at that revision: https://github.com/meshtastic/firmware/blob/87bb149afb9d9e17ef744aad5fbc010c3371f4dc/platformio.ini
- Meshtastic variants tree at that revision: https://github.com/meshtastic/firmware/tree/87bb149afb9d9e17ef744aad5fbc010c3371f4dc/variants
- Meshtastic firmware build docs checked as live docs on 2026-06-30: https://meshtastic.org/docs/development/firmware/build/
- MeshCore repo HEAD checked at e8d3c53ba1ea863937081cd0caad759b832f3028: https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/tree/e8d3c53ba1ea863937081cd0caad759b832f3028
- MeshCore platformio.ini at that revision: https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/blob/e8d3c53ba1ea863937081cd0caad759b832f3028/platformio.ini
- MeshCore variants tree at that revision: https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/tree/e8d3c53ba1ea863937081cd0caad759b832f3028/variants
- MeshCore helper abstractions at that revision: https://github.com/meshcore-dev/MeshCore/tree/e8d3c53ba1ea863937081cd0caad759b832f3028/src/helpers

Conclusion: be strongly inspired by Meshtastic and MeshCore for board taxonomy, known pinouts, target-family coverage, radio/display/input/GNSS/power quirks, build-matrix coverage, and UI/app expectations. Do not copy their HAL architecture directly into LICHEN because both are PlatformIO/Arduino-centered. Meshtastic declares PlatformIO as the firmware build path and its root platformio.ini pulls variants from variants/*/*.ini and nested platformio.ini files. MeshCore platformio.ini defines arduino_base, ESP32/nRF52/RP2040/STM32 bases, PlatformIO platforms/packages, and board variants under variants/.

Reusable patterns to adapt:
- Keep a board capability matrix that mirrors their real supported boards and variant naming where useful.
- Treat display, buttons, GNSS, RTC/time, battery/PMIC, BLE/serial, storage, and radio model as explicit capabilities.
- Capture hardware quirks near board definitions, not in application/protocol code.
- Preserve a small number of reference profiles: display+buttons+GNSS, display+buttons without GNSS, headless/module, and gateway/bridge.
- Use their board lists to prioritize partner handoff and smoke-test coverage.

Patterns to avoid copying:
- PlatformIO env inheritance as the source of truth.
- Arduino global objects, loop/setup lifecycle, and compile-time macro sprawl in application code.
- Variant headers that leak pin and peripheral details across protocol/UI layers.
- RadioLib-specific type assumptions at the LICHEN protocol boundary.

LICHEN mapping:
- Translate PlatformIO variants to Zephyr board definitions, overlays, devicetree chosen/alias nodes, and Kconfig capability symbols.
- Expose capabilities through a LICHEN HAL/service registry so UI, local-client interfaces, location/time providers, and protocol code never need board-specific ifdefs.
- Keep long-tail board overlays and hardware validation partner-owned after our HAL contract, reference boards, and validation matrix are stable.
notes:
User direction: be strongly inspired by Meshtastic and MeshCore HAL/board support if the patterns are sound, but keep LICHEN Zephyr-native. Answered by source check: both upstreams are PlatformIO/Arduino-centered; use them as hardware-reference input and anti-regression data, not as the architecture to copy.

2026-07-01 closure-evidence review round: Kierkegaard found one P2 that two review passes were not yet recorded on this Bead; substantive acceptance was otherwise satisfied and h7t5.1/h7t5.2 linkage was confirmed. Aquinas found two P2s: source refs used moving branch URLs despite recorded SHAs, and two review passes were not recorded. Fixed by replacing GitHub source refs for Meshtastic/MeshCore code with commit-pinned URLs and recording review evidence here. Meshtastic live docs remain explicitly dated as a live documentation check from 2026-06-30 because the content is not repository-pinned in this Bead. Both reviewers confirmed the content otherwise satisfies the substantive criteria: reusable patterns, anti-patterns, LICHEN HAL mapping, h7t5.1 linkage, and h7t5.2 board matrix reflection.
Final closure review round (2026-07-01): Locke reported no P0/P1/P2; confirmed moving upstream refs and missing review-evidence P2s are resolved, h7t5.1/h7t5.2/matrix linkage is covered, and the dated Meshtastic live-docs link is non-blocking. Ramanujan the 2nd independently reported no P0/P1/P2 and closure acceptable; verified commit-pinned source refs, explicit reusable ideas/anti-patterns/HAL mapping, h7t5.1 linkage, h7t5.2 matrix reflection, and adequate review evidence.
close_reason: Completed Meshtastic/MeshCore HAL-pattern evaluation with commit-pinned source refs, reusable patterns, anti-patterns, LICHEN HAL mapping, h7t5.1 and h7t5.2 linkage, and clean final review pair.
